notes.text:
ORTHOPEDIC CONSULTATION NOTE

Patient: Sam Rodriguez | DOB: 1972-09-04 | Sex: M | MRN: EHR-SR-001
Date: 2026-05-05 | Provider: Dr. James Patel, MD | Specialty: Orthopedic Surgery
Place of Service: Office (POS 11)

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT
Right knee pain for 4 months, worsening, not responding to treatment.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS
Mr. Sam Rodriguez is a 53-year-old male presenting with a 4-month history of right knee pain. He describes the pain as aching and sharp with certain movements, particularly with twisting, squatting, and ascending stairs. He reports a popping sensation in the right knee that began approximately 4 months ago during a recreational soccer game. Since that time, he has had persistent pain along the medial joint line, intermittent swelling, and occasional locking/clicking sensations with knee flexion.

Pain severity: 6/10 at baseline, up to 9/10 with provocative activities. Worse with pivoting and prolonged walking. The patient reports that conservative measures have been tried per his primary care physician's recommendation; however, despite these efforts, he has not experienced meaningful improvement in pain or function, and his symptoms have progressively worsened. He has been unable to return to his usual recreational activities.

Note: The patient verbally reports that conservative measures failed per patient report. Formal physical therapy records were not available for review at today's visit. The patient reports participating in approximately 6-8 weeks of home exercise program and NSAID therapy as directed by his PCP.

No history of prior knee surgery. No prior knee injections. No locking requiring manipulation. BMI 27.

---

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY
- Mild hypertension (on low-dose HCTZ)
- No prior musculoskeletal surgeries
- No bleeding disorders

MEDICATIONS
- Hydrochlorothiazide 12.5 mg daily
- Ibuprofen 600 mg TID PRN knee pain (per PCP instructions)
- Naproxen 500 mg BID PRN (previously prescribed, patient reports limited benefit)

ALLERGIES
- NKDA

SOCIAL HISTORY
- Married, works in IT
- Recreational soccer player (unable to play for 4 months due to knee)
- Non-smoker, social alcohol

FAMILY HISTORY
- Mother: osteoarthritis
- Father: diabetes type 2

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS
- Positive: right knee pain, swelling, mechanical clicking/locking sensation, functional limitation
- Negative: fever, constitutional symptoms, bilateral joint complaints, back pain radiating to leg, hip pain

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION

Vital Signs: BP 130/82 mmHg, HR 74 bpm, Weight 185 lbs, Height 5'10"

General: Alert, well-appearing male. Mild antalgic gait favoring the right lower extremity.

Right Knee Examination:
- Inspection: Mild effusion present. No ecchymosis or erythema.
- Palpation: Tenderness to palpation along the medial joint line. Medial collateral ligament area mildly tender. No lateral joint line tenderness.
- Range of Motion: Flexion 0-125 degrees (limited by pain at end range). Extension 0 degrees.
- McMurray Test: Positive — pain and click with valgus stress and external tibial rotation (suggestive of medial meniscal pathology).
- Apley's Compression Test: Positive with internal rotation.
- Lachman Test: Negative.
- Anterior/Posterior Drawer: Negative.
- Valgus/Varus Stress Test: Negative at 0 and 30 degrees.
- Patellar Grind: Negative.
- Ballottement: Mild fluid wave present.

Left Knee: Unremarkable.

Neurovascular: Distal pulses intact. Sensation intact. Motor 5/5 in bilateral lower extremities.

---

ASSESSMENT
Suspected medial meniscal tear, right knee. Physical exam findings are consistent with internal derangement of the right knee — positive McMurray test, medial joint line tenderness, and mild effusion — in a patient with a mechanism consistent with meniscal injury (twisting injury during athletic activity). Conservative measures have failed per patient report, with ongoing functional limitation after 4 months of symptoms.

Diagnosis: Internal derangement, right knee (M23.231 — Derangement of anterior horn of medial meniscus due to old tear or injury, right knee — working diagnosis pending MRI confirmation).

---

PLAN
1. Order MRI right knee without contrast (CPT 73721) — for further evaluation of suspected medial meniscal tear and to rule out associated ligamentous or chondral pathology. Imaging will directly change clinical management: if MRI confirms meniscal tear, patient will be a candidate for arthroscopic meniscal repair vs. partial meniscectomy; if negative, conservative management will be intensified.
2. Continue current anti-inflammatory regimen (naproxen 500 mg BID) while awaiting MRI results.
3. Refer for formal physical therapy evaluation in parallel (pending imaging).
4. Discuss surgical options with patient once MRI results reviewed.

ICD-10 codes: M23.231 (Derangement of anterior horn of medial meniscus due to old tear or injury, right knee) — primary working diagnosis

Signed: Dr. James Patel, MD | Orthopedic Surgery | NPI: 0987654321
Date/Time: 2026-05-05 10:45

PHYSICAL THERAPY DISCHARGE SUMMARY

Patient: Sam Rodriguez | DOB: 1972-09-04 | MRN: EHR-SR-001
PT Provider: Lauren Kim, DPT | Cascade Sports Therapy
Episode of Care: 2026-02-25 to 2026-04-22 (8 weeks)
Total Visits: 12 sessions (1.5x/week per protocol)
Referring MD: Dr. James Patel, Orthopedic Surgery

REASON FOR REFERRAL
Conservative management of suspected right medial meniscal tear. Patient referred for course of structured PT prior to MRI/surgical decision-making.

INITIAL EXAM (2026-02-25)
- Right knee pain 6/10 baseline, 9/10 with stairs, pivoting.
- Mild antalgic gait.
- ROM right knee: flexion 0-115° (limited by pain at end range), extension 0°.
- Strength: quadriceps 4-/5, hamstrings 4/5, hip abductors 3+/5 (weak — contributory).
- Functional limitations: unable to descend stairs reciprocally; unable to squat past 60°; unable to participate in recreational soccer.

INTERVENTION
Progressive resistance program: quadriceps strengthening, hip abductor activation, hamstring lengthening, proprioception/balance training, gait normalization. Modalities: cryotherapy post-session, manual therapy as tolerated.

PROGRESS BY VISIT 6 (mid-trial)
- Pain reduced from 6/10 to 4/10 at rest.
- ROM gains: flexion improved to 0-122°.
- Strength: quad 4/5, hip abd 4-/5.
- BUT: continued mechanical symptoms — clicking and intermittent locking with deep flexion.

DISCHARGE EXAM (2026-04-22)
- Pain remains 5-6/10 with provocative activities (stairs, pivoting, prolonged standing).
- Mechanical symptoms persist: positive McMurray reproduces pain and click. Clinical pattern UNCHANGED from initial.
- Strength: quad 4+/5, hip abd 4/5 — improved but symptoms unchanged.
- Patient unable to return to recreational soccer; functional limitations persist.

ASSESSMENT
Patient has completed an 8-week structured PT trial (12 visits) with documented progressive home exercise compliance. Strength has improved; however, mechanical symptoms (clicking, locking, McMurray-positive findings) and pain with provocative activities have NOT resolved. Pattern is consistent with a structural intra-articular lesion not amenable to conservative management alone.

RECOMMENDATIONS
1. Failed conservative management — refer back to Dr. Patel (Orthopedic Surgery) for advanced imaging (MRI) and surgical consultation.
2. Continue home exercise program for maintenance during workup.
3. Avoid pivoting and high-impact activities until evaluated.

PT trial documented as complete. Patient discharged to physician care.

Signed: Lauren Kim, DPT | Cascade Sports Therapy | License #PT-WA-44128
Date: 2026-04-22

RADIOLOGY REPORT — KNEE X-RAY

Patient: Sam Rodriguez | DOB: 1972-09-04 | MRN: EHR-SR-001
Study: X-ray Right Knee, 4 views (AP, lateral, sunrise, tunnel)
Date of Service: 2026-02-20
Indication: Right knee pain after twisting injury during recreational soccer; assess for fracture, joint effusion, degenerative changes.
Reading Radiologist: Dr. Theresa Lin, MD

FINDINGS
Bony structures: No acute fracture or dislocation. No avulsion fragments. No osseous lesion.
Joint space: Mild medial joint space narrowing of the right knee, otherwise preserved. No significant lateral or patellofemoral joint space loss.
Alignment: Normal mechanical alignment. No varus or valgus deformity.
Soft tissues: Mild suprapatellar effusion noted. Otherwise unremarkable soft tissues.
Patella: Normal position, no patella alta or baja. Normal articular surface on the sunrise view.

IMPRESSION
1. No acute fracture or dislocation of the right knee.
2. Mild medial joint space narrowing — likely reflects early degenerative change, but does not explain the patient's mechanical symptoms.
3. Mild knee effusion, non-specific.
4. RECOMMENDATION: If symptoms persist after a course of conservative management, MRI is recommended to evaluate intra-articular soft tissue structures including the menisci and ligaments.

Signed: Theresa Lin, MD | Diagnostic Radiology
Date: 2026-02-20

MEDICATION TRIAL DOCUMENTATION — RIGHT KNEE PAIN

Patient: Sam Rodriguez | DOB: 1972-09-04 | MRN: EHR-SR-001
Documenting Provider: Dr. Anita Brar, MD — Primary Care
Date: 2026-04-15

PURPOSE
Document course of NSAID and analgesic therapy attempted for right knee pain prior to specialty referral and advanced imaging.

TRIAL HISTORY
1. Ibuprofen 600 mg TID
   - Started: 2026-01-22
   - Duration: 4 weeks
   - Outcome: PARTIAL RELIEF, ~30% pain reduction. Continued mechanical symptoms (clicking, locking with stairs). No GI side effects.
   - Disposition: Continued PRN for breakthrough pain.

2. Naproxen 500 mg BID
   - Started: 2026-02-25
   - Duration: 6 weeks
   - Outcome: MINIMAL ADDITIONAL BENEFIT beyond ibuprofen. Pain remained 5-6/10 with provocative activities. Mechanical symptoms unchanged.
   - Disposition: Discontinued 2026-04-08 — no incremental benefit.

3. Topical diclofenac 1% gel
   - Started: 2026-03-15
   - Duration: 4 weeks (concurrent with PT)
   - Outcome: NO MEANINGFUL BENEFIT. Patient reports brief topical relief but no functional improvement.
   - Disposition: Discontinued.

ADDITIONAL CONSERVATIVE MEASURES
- Activity modification: avoidance of pivoting/cutting sports — followed.
- Cryotherapy post-activity — followed.
- Home exercise program (provided 2026-01-22, prior to formal PT) — followed.
- Weight loss counseling: BMI 27, advised to reduce; no significant change documented.

ASSESSMENT
Patient has had an adequate trial of multiple NSAIDs and conservative measures over ~12 weeks without resolution of mechanical symptoms. Trial includes both selective and non-selective oral NSAIDs and a topical agent. Mechanical symptoms (locking, clicking) on exam are unlikely to respond to further pharmacologic conservative management and are concerning for structural meniscal pathology.

PLAN
Refer to Orthopedic Surgery (Dr. Patel) for evaluation and consideration of MRI.

Signed: Dr. Anita Brar, MD — Primary Care
Date: 2026-04-15
